headline: SentinelOne provides tepid quarterly forecast, to cut 8% jobs
summary: Cybersecurity company SentinelOne forecast second-quarter revenue below analysts' expectations on Thursday and said it would cut ‌about 8% of its workforce as it looks to invest ‌in growth areas such as AI, data and cloud.  • SentinelOne faces intense competition from larger rivals such as CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks, as well as from Microsoft, which is bundling security ‌features into its products.  • ⁠Even as ransomware and nation-state threats boost cybersecurity demand, some corporate clients are tightening their budgets, scrutinizing ⁠deals and extending sales cycles.

headline: CrowdStrike, Salesforce could be AI winners, market expert says
summary: STORY: "The winners will be software companies that integrate AI into their offerings," said Kampitsis, who sees undervalued opportunities in the space.He pointed to cybersecurity names including CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks as companies that are integrating AI into their offerings, saying they "represent an opportunity."Kampitsis said another "opportunity with upside" was Salesforce, which he called an artificial intelligence company of its own."We think there's a lot of potential upside there for an investor looking to take a chance on a real opportunity," he said.Salesforce forecast second-quarter revenue below Wall Street estimates on Wednesday despite a stronger-than-expected first quarter.

headline: Do cybersecurity stocks still have room to run as AI scales up?
summary: Cybersecurity stocks like CrowdStrike (CRWD), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), and SailPoint (SAIL) are in focus as Wall Street leans more bullish into the AI trade, especially around cybersecurity applications. Does the software sector still have some fight in it as these 3 stocks have seen big gains over the last month and in 2026? Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi welcomes a panel consisting of Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré and PNC Asset Management Group CIO Amanda Agati to discuss investor sentiment surrounding the software trade.
